objective: Deliver a persistent loopback-only Hina Dev Console that lets the owner manually exercise the real M01 HTTP, WebSocket, durable replay, binary media, metrics and redacted error-log paths from a browser.
non_goals: Do not simulate an AI conversation, model response, speech engine, avatar, memory or tool execution.
Do not deploy the console publicly or bind any service to a non-loopback address.
Do not add a frontend framework, external CDN asset or new runtime dependency.
Do not run the full M01 release, repeat, soak or 100-cycle workflow during this fast product slice.
contracts: The console is a long-running local application served from the same loopback origin as the M01 control plane.
Every visible event, replay and binary result comes from the real runtime WebSocket connection; the console contains no fake AI response path.
The console serves a fixed static-file allowlist with no directory traversal and a restrictive Content-Security-Policy.
The error endpoint returns at most 100 already-redacted structured error records and never exposes an arbitrary filesystem path.
The metrics endpoint returns only the bounded in-memory MetricRegistry snapshot.
The start command keeps running until Ctrl+C and reports its URL, database and log locations.
allowed_dependencies: Python 3.12+ standard library, browser platform APIs and existing local hina-contracts and hina-core modules.
acceptance_criteria: pnpm start:dev-console starts a persistent application at http://127.0.0.1:8765/ that can be opened and manually tested by the owner.
The owner can inspect health, version, config and metrics, connect WebSocket, submit a valid durable echo event, observe deduplication, replay a stream and round-trip a binary frame.
The owner can deliberately submit an invalid event and inspect the resulting stable redacted error record in the same console.
Unit tests cover static serving, security headers, metrics, bounded redacted errors and application assembly shutdown.
The focused fast test command passes once on the owner machine.
